title: Spiritual Fitness: Heart Check
subtitle: What does your inner man actually look like right now?
burden: Too many believers are spiritually malnourished but don't know it. This message is a diagnostic — a mirror held up to the inner man to reveal what we've been feeding, and what we've been starving.

sections.title: Introduction
sections.content: We go to the gym for the body. We visit the doctor for physical health. But when did you last do a full check on your spirit?

sections.title: The Temple Principle
sections.content: Your body houses the Holy Spirit. You are not the owner — you are the steward. Stewardship begins with maintenance.
sections.application: What are you allowing to enter the temple? Media, conversations, habits — these are spiritual nutrition or toxins.

sections.title: Diagnosing the Inner Man
sections.content: Four diagnostics: prayer life, Word intake, worship posture, and generosity. These four reveal the actual state of your spirit.
sections.application: Run the checklist. Be honest. The goal isn't condemnation — it's calibration.

sections.title: The Discipline of Renewal
sections.content: Renewal is not automatic. It requires intentional, scheduled spiritual practice.
sections.application: Design your week with spiritual disciplines built in. Not when it feels good — by structure.

sections.title: Closing Charge
sections.content: God is not asking for perfection. He's asking for surrender. Give him the whole person — not just Sunday mornings.
sections.application: Create me a clean heart, O God. That prayer is your response.

closingCharge: You are a living temple. Act like it.

title: Faith That Speaks
subtitle: Words carry spiritual authority — are yours aligned?
burden: Faith without voice is incomplete. This message calls believers to align their confession with their conviction — to speak what they believe before they see what they hope for.
closingCharge: Open your mouth. Faith has a voice.

title: God's Design for Dependence
subtitle: Independence is a trap. Learn to lean.
burden: Western culture glorifies self-sufficiency. But the Kingdom works by a different law — those who lean hardest on God move the furthest. This is the paradox of strength through surrender.
closingCharge: The branch that leans hardest into the vine bears the most fruit.

title: Ask, Seek, Knock
subtitle: The architecture of answered prayer
burden: Prayer is not a last resort — it's a first language. This message rebuilds the framework of how believers approach God in petition.
closingCharge: Ask. Don't assume. He said ask.

title: Walking in Purpose
subtitle: Your assignment was written before you were born
burden: Purpose is not a destination you arrive at — it's a posture you walk in. This message dismantles the anxiety of the unlived calling and repositions believers toward active obedience.
closingCharge: You are a workmanship. Start walking like it.

title: Love in Action
subtitle: What 1 Corinthians 13 actually demands of you
burden: We quote 1 Corinthians 13 at weddings but don't live it Monday through Saturday. This message makes the love chapter personal, practical, and unavoidable.
closingCharge: Love is not a feeling — it's a decision, renewed daily.
